message:
Add multi-client blocking command conformance harness (frankenredis-w1y3)

Extends the multi-client fixture format with send_only and read_pending
fields for testing blocking command wake-up scenarios (BLPOP, BRPOP,
BZPOPMIN, BZPOPMAX). When send_only=true, commands are sent without
waiting for response; read_pending=true reads the pending response after
another client triggers the wake-up.

Changes:
- MultiClientStep: add send_only, read_pending, blocking_timeout_ms fields
- run_live_redis_multi_client_diff: track pending responses and handle
  blocking read pattern
- New fixture: core_blocking_multi_client.json with cross-client scenarios
- Updated live_oracle_matrix.json with multi_client profile entry
- Fixed pre-existing clippy lints (collapsible_if, is_multiple_of,
  single_char_add_str, if_same_then_else)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"frankenredis-mmg","title":"Implement SHUTDOWN graceful server termination","description":"## Problem

fr-command/src/lib.rs line 10729: SHUTDOWN command returns OK but does not actually shut down the server. The comment says: \"Stub -- in production this would trigger graceful shutdown\".

## What Redis Does

SHUTDOWN [NOSAVE | SAVE] [NOW] [FORCE] [ABORT]:
1. Optionally triggers a final SAVE (RDB snapshot)
2. Flushes AOF if configured
3. Closes all client connections gracefully
4. Exits the process

SHUTDOWN ABORT cancels a pending shutdown.
SHUTDOWN NOSAVE skips the final snapshot.

## Implementation Plan

Since SHUTDOWN is a runtime-special command, it needs to signal the server event loop to begin graceful termination:

1. Add a shutdown_requested flag to ServerState
2. SHUTDOWN handler sets the flag (and optionally triggers SAVE)
3. fr-server main loop checks the flag each tick
4. On shutdown: flush writes, close connections, drop listener, exit

## Files to Modify

- crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs: ServerState flag, SHUTDOWN handler logic
- crates/fr-server/src/main.rs: Check flag in main loop, graceful exit","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:10:45.912067485Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:45:05.661284398Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:45:05.660779813Z","close_reason":"SHUTDOWN: sets shutdown_requested flag, server event loop exits gracefully with optional final SAVE","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
@@ -307,6 +308,7 @@
 {"id":"frankenredis-vmw","title":"Implement cjson.encode and cjson.decode in Lua evaluator","description":"## Problem

fr-command/src/lua_eval.rs line 1565: cjson is stubbed with a comment \"cjson stub (commonly used in Redis scripts)\". The cjson library is one of the most commonly used libraries in Redis Lua scripts for JSON serialization/deserialization.

## What Redis Provides

Redis bundles cjson (lua-cjson) which provides:
- cjson.encode(value) → JSON string
- cjson.decode(json_string) → Lua value
- cjson.encode_sparse_array(value) → JSON with sparse array handling

Common usage pattern:
  local data = cjson.decode(redis.call('GET', 'user:1'))
  data.visits = data.visits + 1
  redis.call('SET', 'user:1', cjson.encode(data))

## Implementation Plan

Since we already depend on serde_json in fr-conformance, we can add it to fr-command:

1. cjson.encode: Convert LuaValue → serde_json::Value → JSON string
   - LuaValue::Nil → JSON null
   - LuaValue::Bool → JSON bool
   - LuaValue::Number → JSON number
   - LuaValue::Str → JSON string (UTF-8)
   - LuaValue::Table → JSON object or array (based on table structure)

2. cjson.decode: Parse JSON string → serde_json::Value → LuaValue
   - JSON null → LuaValue::Nil
   - JSON bool → LuaValue::Bool
   - JSON number → LuaValue::Number
   - JSON string → LuaValue::Str
   - JSON array → LuaValue::Table (array part)
   - JSON object → LuaValue::Table (hash part)

## Edge Cases

- Empty table: encode as {} (object) or [] (array)? Redis cjson defaults to object.
- Mixed table (both array and hash keys): Redis cjson errors on this.
- Very large/deep structures: need recursion depth limit
- Non-UTF-8 strings: cjson.encode errors on these

## Files to Modify

- crates/fr-command/Cargo.toml: Add serde_json dependency
- crates/fr-command/src/lua_eval.rs: Replace stubs with real implementations","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:08:24.155327935Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T04:57:49.062059532Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T04:57:49.061162918Z","close_reason":"cjson.encode/decode already fully implemented with custom JSON serializer. Fixed misleading 'stub' comment.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankenredis-vmw","depends_on_id":"frankenredis-7vn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:08:27.928473121Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"frankenredis-vw1n","title":"List GETKEYSINSLOT and COUNTKEYSINSLOT in CLUSTER HELP","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-31T17:40:46.441767800Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-31T17:43:51.398679390Z","closed_at":"2026-03-31T17:43:51.398418657Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenredis-w1m","title":"Wire CONFIG SET busy-reply-threshold (lua-time-limit) to runtime","description":"## Problem

fr-runtime/src/lib.rs line 1294 defines `const COMMAND_TIME_BUDGET_MS: u128 = 5000`. This matches Redis's busy-reply-threshold default (5000ms) but is not configurable.

## What Redis Does

CONFIG SET busy-reply-threshold <milliseconds> controls when Redis starts rejecting commands with BUSY errors during long-running scripts or operations. CONFIG SET lua-time-limit is the legacy name.

## Implementation Plan

1. Add command_time_budget_ms to ServerState (initialized to 5000)
2. Handle CONFIG SET busy-reply-threshold and lua-time-limit (both set the same value)
3. Replace the const usage with the runtime field

## Files to Modify

- crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs: Add field, CONFIG SET handler, replace const","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:05:16.267291289Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:42:13.058081750Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:42:13.056821354Z","close_reason":"busy-reply-threshold/lua-time-limit wired: ServerState.command_time_budget_ms + CONFIG SET/GET + replaces const","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankenredis-w1m","depends_on_id":"frankenredis-kgn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:05:19.891477646Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

@@ -85,6 +85,24 @@ Example multi-client fixture format:
 }
 ```
 
+Blocking command wake-up testing uses `send_only` and `read_pending` fields:
+```json
+{
+  "suite": "core_blocking_multi_client",
+  "clients": ["blocker", "pusher"],
+  "steps": [
+    { "name": "blocker_blpop", "client": "blocker", "argv": ["BLPOP", "key", "5"], "send_only": true },
+    { "name": "pusher_rpush", "client": "pusher", "argv": ["RPUSH", "key", "val"], "expect": {...} },
+    { "name": "blocker_receives", "client": "blocker", "read_pending": true, "blocking_timeout_ms": 5000, "expect": {...} }
+  ]
+}
+```
+
+Step fields:
+- `send_only: true` - send command but don't wait for response (for blocking commands)
+- `read_pending: true` - read pending response from previous `send_only` step
+- `blocking_timeout_ms` - timeout for `read_pending` response (default: 5000ms)
+
 Topology-heavy suites (replication chains, cluster) remain outside the parity matrix for now.
 Those surfaces include `core_replication`, `core_wait`, `core_migrate`, and the packet-006
 replication fixtures.

@@ -0,0 +1,152 @@
+{
+  "suite": "core_blocking_multi_client",
+  "clients": ["blocker", "pusher"],
+  "steps": [
+    {
+      "name": "setup_flushdb",
+      "now_ms": 0,
+      "client": "pusher",
+      "argv": ["FLUSHDB"],
+      "expect": {
+        "kind": "simple",
+        "value": "OK"
+      }
+    },
+    {
+      "name": "blocker_sends_blpop",
+      "now_ms": 10,
+      "client": "blocker",
+      "argv": ["BLPOP", "wake_key", "5"],
+      "send_only": true
+    },
+    {
+      "name": "pusher_pushes_data",
+      "now_ms": 100,
+      "client": "pusher",
+      "argv": ["RPUSH", "wake_key", "wakeup_value"],
+      "expect": {
+        "kind": "integer",
+        "value": 1
+      }
+    },
+    {
+      "name": "blocker_receives_blpop_result",
+      "now_ms": 200,
+      "client": "blocker",
+      "read_pending": true,
+      "blocking_timeout_ms": 5000,
+      "expect": {
+        "kind": "array",
+        "value": [
+          { "kind": "bulk", "value": "wake_key" },
+          { "kind": "bulk", "value": "wakeup_value" }
+        ]
+      }
+    },
+    {
+      "name": "blocker_sends_brpop",
+      "now_ms": 300,
+      "client": "blocker",
+      "argv": ["BRPOP", "brpop_key", "5"],
+      "send_only": true
+    },
+    {
+      "name": "pusher_pushes_brpop_data",
+      "now_ms": 400,
+      "client": "pusher",
+      "argv": ["RPUSH", "brpop_key", "first", "second", "third"],
+      "expect": {
+        "kind": "integer",
+        "value": 3
+      }
+    },
+    {
+      "name": "blocker_receives_brpop_result",
+      "now_ms": 500,
+      "client": "blocker",
+      "read_pending": true,
+      "blocking_timeout_ms": 5000,
+      "expect": {
+        "kind": "array",
+        "value": [
+          { "kind": "bulk", "value": "brpop_key" },
+          { "kind": "bulk", "value": "third" }
+        ]
+      }
+    },
+    {
+      "name": "blocker_sends_bzpopmin",
+      "now_ms": 600,
+      "client": "blocker",
+      "argv": ["BZPOPMIN", "zset_key", "5"],
+      "send_only": true
+    },
+    {
+      "name": "pusher_adds_zset_members",
+      "now_ms": 700,
+      "client": "pusher",
+      "argv": ["ZADD", "zset_key", "10", "member1", "5", "member2", "15", "member3"],
+      "expect": {
+        "kind": "integer",
+        "value": 3
+      }
+    },
+    {
+      "name": "blocker_receives_bzpopmin_result",
+      "now_ms": 800,
+      "client": "blocker",
+      "read_pending": true,
+      "blocking_timeout_ms": 5000,
+      "expect": {
+        "kind": "array",
+        "value": [
+          { "kind": "bulk", "value": "zset_key" },
+          { "kind": "bulk", "value": "member2" },
+          { "kind": "bulk", "value": "5" }
+        ]
+      }
+    },
+    {
+      "name": "blocker_sends_bzpopmax",
+      "now_ms": 900,
+      "client": "blocker",
+      "argv": ["BZPOPMAX", "zset_key", "5"],
+      "send_only": true
+    },
+    {
+      "name": "pusher_triggers_bzpopmax_wakeup",
+      "now_ms": 950,
+      "client": "pusher",
+      "argv": ["ZADD", "zset_key", "99", "highest"],
+      "expect": {
+        "kind": "integer",
+        "value": 1
+      }
+    },
+    {
+      "name": "blocker_receives_bzpopmax_result",
+      "now_ms": 1000,
+      "client": "blocker",
+      "read_pending": true,
+      "blocking_timeout_ms": 5000,
+      "expect": {
+        "kind": "array",
+        "value": [
+          { "kind": "bulk", "value": "zset_key" },
+          { "kind": "bulk", "value": "highest" },
+          { "kind": "bulk", "value": "99" }
+        ]
+      }
+    },
+    {
+      "name": "cleanup",
+      "now_ms": 1100,
+      "client": "pusher",
+      "argv": ["FLUSHDB"],
+      "expect": {
+        "kind": "simple",
+        "value": "OK"
+      }
+    }
+  ]
+}
